Continuity Web
Failure Mode Manifest Quarantine Governance
Trust succession for catastrophic compromise

Fork authority, not the internet

Continuity Web preserves legitimate digital authority when identity, updates, routing, clouds, and AI delegations can no longer be trusted as a shared command environment.

Read the brief See the mechanism
Infrastructure remains data Old services may stay reachable without retaining command power.
Authority becomes epochal Credentials are evaluated under declared trust-epoch policy.
Succession is public Manifests, stamps, and quorums make fork claims inspectable.
01 - the gap

When valid commands stop proving legitimacy

Catastrophic compromise is not just downtime. The harder failure is authority ambiguity: commands, credentials, agents, and update roots still look valid after the trust environment is contaminated.

Reachability is not legitimacy

Packets may route, domains may resolve, and services may answer while the right to command is no longer knowable.

Machine-speed delegation changes the clock

AI systems can act through normal channels faster than human operators can interpret, revoke, or coordinate.

The missing primitive is succession

Backups restore state. Continuity Web asks which authority is admissible after a contaminated epoch.

Continuity Web does not replace incident response. It gives legitimate actors a way to leave a poisoned command layer behind.

The old network can remain useful as evidence, history, and transport. It should not automatically authenticate, update, govern, delegate, or command the successor network.
02 - succession

A successor epoch starts from a signed manifest

01

Declare the last attestable checkpoint The fork begins from evidence that independent parties can inspect.

02

Replace contaminated roots New identity, provenance, namespace, attestation, and governance roots are explicit.

03

Admit by proof, not reputation Participants prove policy compliance with fresh keys and privacy-preserving nullifiers.

03 - boundary

Import evidence. Do not import command.

Quarantine bridges let old-epoch data cross as content-addressed, sandboxed, read-only evidence. Credentials, executable permissions, updates, and governance claims do not cross by default.

Old data can be valuable

Logs, records, and snapshots are imported as evidence only after sanitization and successor re-signing.

Old authority is dangerous

Admin rights, update channels, agent keys, and command paths must earn new admissibility.

AI authority expires at succession

Capability passports bind owner, tools, limits, expiry, delegation depth, and epoch survivability.

04 - legitimacy

No single actor gets to declare the new web

A clean fork requires quorum-of-quorums governance: independent technical validators, public institutions, operators, auditors, hardware-root diversity, open-source maintainers, and recovery guardians.

Stamps are scoped claims

They commit to manifest hash, evidence root, checkpoint, quorum root, validator set, and status.

Regions can operate locally

Provisional, justified, finalized, contested, and reconciled states reduce announcement wars.

Modes degrade before they fork

High-risk authority can freeze, AI delegation can expire, and updates can restrict before full succession.

01Increase logging
02Freeze high-risk authority
03Revoke AI delegation
04Restrict updates
05Regional continuity
06Full succession
Consortium target

Build the minimum credible succession demo.

The first program should not replace the internet. It should prove succession for a bounded community: a software ecosystem, university network, emergency-response consortium, or critical-infrastructure simulation.

Open the technical report
Continuity Web is a proposed open research program for recoverable legitimacy under catastrophic cyber compromise.
